title: Responses, Problem Details, Streaming, and Protocol-Aware Output · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A server pairing session predicts request, handler, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before building the first single-commit response and streaming contract for responses, problem details, streaming, and protocol-aware output. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of single-commit response and streaming contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Predict implicit 200, content sniffing, automatic length, one final status, informational responses, and header commitment order.
Serialize RFC 9457 problem type, title, status, detail, instance, extensions, and application/problem+json without leaking implementation data.
Map validation, authentication, authorization, absence, conflict, precondition, limit, timeout, cancellation, dependency, and internal failures consistently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Writeheader Implicit Commit
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 8171 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose one response shape and bound streaming flush evidence; then cancel during the dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Predict implicit 200, content sniffing, automatic length, one final status, informational responses, and header commitment order.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Stop stream producers on context cancellation, bound queues and bytes, preserve partial-response evidence, and avoid goroutine or resource leaks.

At Go server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Stream Backpressure Cleanup, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop team handles case 8180 in a civic incident API behind one trusted regional proxy. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose one response shape and bound streaming flush evidence; then saturate one admission budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
